hero.subtitle: Understand JWT authentication, the role system, and how to use decorators and guards.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The system uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for authentication and a roles system for authorization. All routes are protected by default except those marked with the @Public() decorator.

login.title: 1. User Login
login.description: The user sends credentials (email and password) to the login endpoint

validateCredentials.title: 2. Credential Validation
validateCredentials.description: AuthService validates email and password using Argon2id to compare the password hash

generateTokens.title: 3. Token Generation
generateTokens.description: The system generates two JWT tokens with base TTL + random jitter:
generateTokens.accessToken: Access Token: 1h base (configurable) + up to JWT_JITTER_SECONDS

useToken.title: 4. Using the Token
useToken.description: The client includes the Access Token in the Authorization header of all requests

validateToken.title: 5. Token Validation
validateToken.description: JwtAuthGuard intercepts the request, extracts, and validates the token using the Passport JWT strategy

refreshToken.title: 6. Refresh Token
refreshToken.description: When the Access Token expires, use the Refresh Token to obtain a new Access Token

verifyToken.title: Verify token
verifyToken.description: Check whether the Access Token is still valid with POST /api/v1/auth/verify-token. Requires the Authorization: Bearer header.

jitter.title: JWT Jitter
jitter.description: Jitter is a random variation added to the token expiration time (TTL) at sign time. The goal is to spread exp across clients that log in at the same time and reduce simultaneous refresh spikes (thundering herd).

configuration.title: Configuration
items: Applied to access and refresh tokens
0 disables jitter

example.title: Practical example
example.intro: With 1h base and 60 jitter:

alert.title: Not clock skew
alert.description: Jitter only changes TTL at token issuance. Passport validation continues with ignoreExpiration: false and no extra leeway.

registration.title: Account creation
registration.description: Register a user with POST /api/v1/auth/register. The account starts inactive until email confirmation; JWT tokens are already returned (with jitter).

conflict.title: Duplicate email / username
conflict.description: If the email (or username) is already registered, the API responds with 409 Conflict instead of 400.

alert.title: Email verification
alert.description: The verification link/token arrives via SMTP. In local Docker, open MailHog at http://localhost:8025. Confirm with GET /api/v1/auth/verify-email?token=....

passwordReset.title: Password reset
passwordReset.intro: Two-step flow: request the recovery email, then set the new password with the received token.

step2.title: 2. Set new password
step2.description: Use the token from the email (in Docker: MailHog at http://localhost:8025).

roles.title: Role System
roles.description: Access is controlled by the {role} enum. Each user can have multiple roles; the field is optional, but values must belong to the enum.

suggestedRoles.title: Role enum values
suggestedRoles.intro: These values are defined in src/modules/auth/enums/role.enum.ts. For new roles, extend the enum — do not use free-form strings.

modelStructure.title: Role structure in the model
modelStructure.description: The users module types roles as Role[]. See also the Users page.

public.label: @Public()
public.description: Marks a route as public, allowing access without authentication. Useful for login, registration, and similar endpoints.

roles.label: @Roles()
roles.description: Defines which roles are required to access the route. The user must have at least one of the specified roles.

jwtAuthGuard.label: JwtAuthGuard
jwtAuthGuard.description: Validates the JWT token on requests: checks that it is valid and not expired, and exposes the payload on req.user. Routes with @Public() are allowed.

rolesGuard.label: RolesGuard
rolesGuard.description: Validates that the authenticated user has at least one of the roles required by @Roles().

tokenPayload.title: Token Payload
tokenPayload.description: The JWT token carries the payload below after authentication. The data is available on req.user.

googleOAuth.title: Social authentication
googleOAuth.description: Google, Facebook, and X / Twitter OAuth2 use Authorization Code Exchange — JWTs are never placed in the redirect URL. Full setup, API reference, and web/mobile examples live under Social Authentication.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.description: JWT settings are configured through environment variables and accessed via ConfigService.

alert.title: Security
alert.description: Use strong, unique secrets in production. JWT_SECRET should be a long, random string. Never commit secrets to the repository.

samples.withoutRoles: // Without @Roles() → any authenticated user can access
samples.needsAdminOrManager: // User must have Role.ADMIN OR Role.MANAGER (OR)
samples.forAndCustomGuard: // For AND, create a custom guard
